strategy_canvas.headline_thesis: Altru wins the wedding-charity intersection by combining four axes that no single competitor crosses: low couple fee, 250% IRAS tax relief routing, PayNow-native guest experience, and wedding-day-purposed UX. Wedding registries cover the wedding axis but lack tax relief; charity platforms cover tax relief but not the wedding flow; PayNow apps cover the rail but route nothing to IPCs by default.

attack_plans.niche_name: Values-led couple × IPC tax-relief × wedding-day flow
attack_plans.icp: SG dual-income couple, 30-38, household income SGD 180-260k, hosting 60-150 guest hotel/restaurant wedding, IRAS-itemising, recoiling from "another set of toaster ovens".
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: SAM-derived: ~25% of the 16,000 banqueted SG marriages/yr are values-led at higher HHI × SGD 60k typical ang bao yield × 22% would route via Altru = ~SGD 22M annual GMV; SOM 18-month subset.
attack_plans.why_gap: Heatmap shows no competitor scores ≥4 across all three needs simultaneously: low fee + IPC routing + wedding-day flow. Wedding registries lack tax relief; charity platforms lack the wedding moment; PayNow apps lack IPC routing.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Altru is the only product that closes all three. The structural defense is the IRAS 250% relief math: a guest at 22% marginal rate giving SGD 200 nets SGD 110 in tax savings — no registry can replicate this.

gtm.channel: Hotel banquet sales partnerships (Marina Bay Sands, Capella, Mandarin Oriental); wedding planners that the values-led ICP already books (One Olive, The Bridal Atelier).
gtm.pitch: Your wedding pays your favourite charity. Your guests get a 250% tax receipt. You skip the gift-list awkwardness.
gtm.pricing: Standard tier: 5% couple-side fee on raise (matches pricing-strategy.json Standard tier).
gtm.content: Co-branded charity-couple stories ("Why we chose Project Dignity for our wedding"); hotel-partnered case studies; Anchor anchor: 250% relief calculator widget.

attack_plans.icp: SG couples 28-40 hosting church / mosque / temple-anchored weddings; existing tithing/sadaqah/seva habit; HHI SGD 80-180k; 80-200 guests across the cultural community.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: ~30% of SG marriages have a religious-community frame; many already direct ang bao to religious charity. SGD 9.5M = SAM × 30% × 18-month adoption ramp.
attack_plans.why_gap: Heatmap shows MUIS/HEB/church portals score high on cultural fit but low on wedding-day integration; Altru can layer the wedding flow on top of pre-existing IPC charity choice.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Altru lets the couple pre-select an MUIS/HEB/church-affiliated IPC and inherit cultural permission, while delivering the table-QR experience guests expect at the ceremony.

gtm.channel: MUIS Wedding Solemniser network (~600 SG); HEB temple wedding-coordinator; church wedding-coordinator forums (Rosaryhill, Wesley, City Harvest).
gtm.pitch: Make your wedding a sadaqah / ministry / seva. We route it through MUIS/HEB/church-approved IPC charities so guests get the IRAS receipt.
gtm.pricing: Free Solemnisation tier (cap SGD 5k raise) for intimate religious solemnisations; Standard 5% above cap.
gtm.content: Religious leader endorsements; tea-ceremony-compatible flow; multi-language receipts.

attack_plans.niche_name: Cross-border SG-MY couple × tax route × dual-flow
attack_plans.icp: One spouse SG citizen, one MY citizen; SG-side ceremony with mixed guest list; SG-tax-resident donors want IRAS receipt; MY-side donors want LHDN equivalent or no receipt.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: ~8% of SG marriages are cross-border SG-MY (~1,800/yr); avg ang bao yield SGD 40k due to smaller MY guest contributions; 25% adoption ceiling = SGD 4.8M annual.
attack_plans.why_gap: Cell heatmap: no competitor scores ≥4 on cultural fit AND wedding-day integration for cross-border. Bridestory (ID), Simply Giving (HK/MY/SG), Honeyfund (US) — none route IPC.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Altru routes SG-tax-resident donors to SG IPCs (250% relief) and offers a "cultural-courtesy" cash-equivalent path for non-resident MY guests with the same QR.

gtm.channel: SG-MY wedding planners (Wedded Wonderland SG); SGCC + MICCI corporate sponsors; cross-border immigration lawyers (downstream wedding-cert clients).
gtm.pitch: One QR. SG guests get the 250% receipt. MY guests give a charity ang bao without paperwork.
gtm.pricing: White-Glove SGD 1,500 flat fee (matches pricing-strategy.json White-Glove tier) — cross-border guest list complexity warrants the flat anchor.
gtm.content: Bilingual landing pages (BM/EN); cross-border tax FAQ; partner with charities operating both SG and MY (Mercy Relief, World Vision).

attack_plans.icp: SG-listed or MNC employer (>500 staff) sending senior management as wedding guests; ESG / community-investment dollar already budgeted; CSR officer wants matched giving as employee benefit.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: ~15% of large-ballroom SG weddings have ≥5 corporate guests; corporate match programs typically 1:1 to SGD 300/employee/event; SGD 3.2M = SAM × adoption × 12-month corp sales ramp.
attack_plans.why_gap: Heatmap shows no competitor combines wedding-day integration with corporate-match infrastructure. Bountie/Whydonate offer corp match but not weddings; Wishlist offers weddings but not corp match.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Altru can flag a guest as "company X employee" via QR scan and trigger employer match in real time, while crediting the couple's impact dashboard. Wedding-day visibility for the CSR program is a unique moment of brand impression.

gtm.channel: CSR officer outbound at SGCC, IHRP networks; Elitez Group portfolio cross-sell (existing corporate clients); StanChart / DBS / OCBC Wealth Management referral.
gtm.pitch: Your senior team is already at this wedding. Match their ang bao to the couple's charity. Your CSR program gets a wedding-day moment of brand goodness.
gtm.pricing: White-Glove SGD 1,500 flat — corporate ESG dollars are not fee-elastic; flat anchor wins.
gtm.content: CSR-officer collateral; Inland-Revenue Form-C compliance memo; case study post-launch.

_legacy.notes: 8x8 overview for SG wedding red-packet-to-charity platform. Segments span couple profile x religion/culture x wedding scale. Needs mix tax-efficiency, wedding flow integration, charity breadth, transparency.

us_scores_rationale.Low couple fee (%): 5% platform fee (couple-side) with 95% to charity — among the lowest in SG charity-tech
us_scores_rationale.250% IRAS tax relief: Direct IPC routing confirms 250% tax relief for each guest donor — structural advantage
us_scores_rationale.PayNow native: QR-code ang bao on the envelope matches existing ritual
us_scores_rationale.Wedding-specific UX: Purpose-built for SG wedding ceremony day; couple dashboard + guest view + tabletop QR
us_scores_rationale.IPC charity breadth: Partnership with ~60 IPC charities at launch; will scale
us_scores_rationale.Guest donor experience: Scan + donate + get e-receipt in 30 sec — frictionless at the wedding table
us_scores_rationale.Corporate / company match: Not yet — deliberate v2 scope after couple-side product-market fit
us_scores_rationale.Couple dashboard & analytics: Real-time donation tally, charity impact thank-you, post-wedding report
